text:
Have-Nots (, Fuka Ji-min—lit. "People who must not be carried") are the lowest caste of people in the Kakin Empire. Most of Camilla's allotted fifteen employees on board the Black Whale are Have-Nots. Hunter × Hunter, Chapter 389 

Overview

Have-Nots were historically forbidden from holding public office or holding rank in the military. Second Prince Camilla Hui Guo Rou gave all the Have-Nots the opportunity to join her personal army, constructed a special ward on her private property, and gave them the same status and rights as the armed forces. 

Background

In ancient Kakin, there existed a sacrificial burial custom known as "afterlife companions." These companions would be buried with Princes who had been unable to become King, with the hope that they would supervise them in the afterlife and prevent them from turning into a vengeful ghost that cursed Kakin or its King. To be chosen was considered an honor, but the companions were exclusively selected from the Have-Nots. The practice eventually ended, but Kakin's immutable caste system remained, and the Have-Nots continued to hold the lowest position, unable to hold public office or rank in the military. After being supported by Prince Camilla, the Have-Nots spontaneously called for the reinstatement of afterlife companions. 

Plot

Succession Contest arc

Upon hearing gunfire shots at the entrance corridor inside Room 1002, some of Prince Camilla's guards (Sarahell, Nukuocon, Hignori, Bucket, and Umanma) rush over, knowing that Prince Camilla and 1 st Prince Benjamin's soldier Musse were there, but find the doors locked. They make their way through another entrance and manage to kick another door down to find Camilla unharmed, Musse missing, and blood on the floor and on Camilla's clothes. Camilla tells them that Musse had shot her and escaped, that she was going to chase him, and instructs her men to burn his personal belongings. Sarahell tries to dissuade Camilla from going after him, believing it would play into the enemy's hands, and calm her down, but Camilla insists on going. Sarahell urges Second Queen Duazul, Camilla's mother, to talk her out of it, but Duazul sides with her daughter and asks Camilla's men to stay behind and let her do as she wishes. Hunter × Hunter - Volume 36, Chapter 373 

Camilla's men are revealed to be powerful curse assassins, through a common Nen ability inspired by their background as afterlife companions. The targets of each Have-Not on board as part of Camilla's retinue are revealed, each in charge of the curse of another Prince. They also possess a Nen Exorcist in case Camilla herself should get cursed. The two Have-Nots previously assigned to curse Momoze and Salé-salé are ordered to determine whether there are any Exorcists among the enemy. After consulting with Fukataki and realizing the difficulty of dealing with the Princes' Nen beasts, Sarahell decides to attend the next round of Nen classes in Woble's quarters, where she plans to curse the Prince. 

Unique Nen Ability

Excepting Fukataki and Umanma (an Exorcist), all Have-Nots shown thus far have the same Nen ability.

Trivia

In the original text, "Have-Nots" is a neologism, with only one kanji of difference from the Japanese word for the Indian Dalit (不可持民, "people who must not be carried", instead of 不可触民, "people who must not be touched"), on which the fictional caste is obviously based.
Curiously Camilla's Nen ability also requires death to activate, utilizing post-mortem Nen. 

Intertextuality and References

The furigana name of the Have-Nots' curse is derived from Japanese mythology, specifically to Izanami's inability to leave the underworld due to having eaten of its food.
The kanji name of the curse, "Tsujitsuma Awase ni Umareta Bokura", is derived from a song by Amazarashi.
Viz localized the curse's name as "Dust in the Wind: Hell Fruit".
